mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals tightening labor market and rising wage pressures, which compress corporate profit margins and reduce equity valuations. This negative earnings outlook produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 6.2% suggests labor market slack remains
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tightening labor conditions
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE declines by > 0.2pp month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales signal strengthening consumer demand and economic momentum, which increases corporate revenue expectations and equity valuations. This positive demand shock produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of instances when RSAFS growth exceeds 0.5% month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate crossing above 0.15% signals the beginning of a monetary tightening cycle. This rate increase raises discount rates applied to future equity cash flows and increases borrowing costs for corporations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 0.09% remains in accommodation zone
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.15%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window following trigger activation.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth combined with declining unemployment signals supply-side capacity constraints and inflationary pressure. When INDPRO growth exceeds 0.3% month-over-month while UNRATE falls below 6.0%, the Fed tightens policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 104.39 and UNRATE at 6.2%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 58% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between INDPRO and UNRATE is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline by more than 5% month-over-month signals weakening construction demand and forward-looking economic pessimism. This demand contraction reduces corporate capital expenditure expectations and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 62% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth combined with rising inflation signals wage-price spiral risk. When PAYEMS growth exceeds 0.2% month-over-month and CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 0.3% month-over-month, the Fed tightens policy, reducing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 139004 thousand and CPIAUCSL at 237.69
regime_hint: inflationary expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between PAYEMS and CPIAUCSL is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPI inflation) turning negative signals monetary accommodation. This negative real rate reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and increases equity relative attractiveness, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 0.09% with low inflation environment
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with low real rates
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with accelerating retail sales signals robust aggregate demand and earnings growth. When both GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0% annualized and RSAFS growth exceeds 0.4% month-over-month, equity valuations expand and produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 2.0% annualized AND RSAFS growth > 0.4% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 15985.7 billion chained 2017 dollars and RSAFS at 439802 million
regime_hint: sustained economic expansion with strong demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 58% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between GDPC1 and RSAFS is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in any 3-year rolling window.
